title: commiphora elliptica
intro: Commiphora elliptica, also known as African myrrh, is a plant species that belongs to the Burseraceae family and is native to tropical Africa. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its resin, which has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including wound healing, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Commiphora elliptica include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting overall well-being. Herbal preparations of Commiphora elliptica include its resin being used in teas, tinctures, and topical ointments, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
